name: Operation Watchman
subtitle: 90-Day Men's Formation
scripture_focus: New Testament (Matthew → Revelation) anchored chronologically: Gospels → Acts → Epistles → Revelation walked through each virtue across the 90 days. Proverbs 1-31 opens the first month in Morning Wisdom; Psalm 119 stanzas occupy the wisdom slot through the NT phase; Evening Peace cycles the remaining Psalter.
notes: v3 curriculum. Each watch rotates through a named acronym framework (REAL MAN / HHAAPPY / FULFILLED / RESOLUTE). Within each virtue's passage bank, references are sorted by canonical NT order so cycles progress from the Gospels through the Epistles. Generic phrasing — a future personalization layer will substitute the user's wife, children, city, state, and nation.

disciplines.title: Cold Shower
disciplines.theme: Discipline the body, strengthen the mind

disciplines.title: Fasting
disciplines.theme: Skip a meal · Fast from distraction

days.reflection_prompt: What post has God assigned you today? What does it mean to stand there?

morning_wisdom.note: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge.

husbands_post.note: Love her as Christ loved the church.

fathers_charge.note: Teach them diligently to your children.

citizens_stand.note: Be not conformed to this world.

evening_peace.note: Blessed is the man who walks not in the counsel of the wicked.

days.reflection_prompt: Where did you compromise today? Where did you hold the line?

days.reflection_prompt: When she spoke, did you actually change — or did you do what you already planned?

citizens_stand.note: The Word of God is quick, and powerful, and sharper than any two-edged sword.

days.reflection_prompt: Where are you leading your family — and do they know?

days.reflection_prompt: Whose name did you carry to God today that does not yet know Him?

morning_wisdom.note: Genuine love. Fervent in spirit. Serving the Lord.

husbands_post.note: Her children rise and call her blessed. Her husband also — he praises her.

fathers_charge.note: Comfort yourselves together, and edify one another.

citizens_stand.note: Walk in wisdom toward them that are without. Redeeming the time.

evening_peace.note: It is a good thing to give thanks unto the LORD. To shew forth Thy lovingkindness in the morning.

days.reflection_prompt: Whose fault did you blame today that was actually yours?

morning_wisdom.note: Let Thy mercies come unto me, O LORD. So shall I have wherewith to answer him that reproacheth me.

husbands_post.note: He who says he abides in Him ought himself to walk as He walked.

fathers_charge.note: I have no greater joy than to hear that my children walk in truth.

citizens_stand.note: A friend loveth at all times. A brother is born for adversity.

evening_peace.note: O come, let us worship and bow down. Let us kneel before the LORD our maker.

days.reflection_prompt: When was the last time you looked at your wife the way you did when you were dating?

morning_wisdom.note: I count not my life dear to myself — that I may finish my course with joy.

husbands_post.note: Set me as a seal upon thine heart. Love is strong as death.

fathers_charge.note: As for me and my house, we will serve the LORD.

citizens_stand.note: Be likeminded, having the same love, being of one accord, of one mind.

evening_peace.note: Sing unto the LORD a new song. Sing unto the LORD, all the earth.

days.reflection_prompt: What does each of your children need from you that no one else can give them?

morning_wisdom.note: He who is faithful in least is faithful in much. You cannot serve two masters.

husbands_post.note: He shall cover thee with His feathers. His truth shall be thy shield.

evening_peace.note: Sing unto the LORD a new song. For He hath done marvellous things.

days.reflection_prompt: Where did your city, state, or nation need a Christian presence today — and were you there?

husbands_post.note: Go to the ant, thou sluggard. Consider her ways and be wise.
